Running your company off statistics is going to save you a lot of time, energy, and stress when it comes to making decisions like firing, hiring, making decisions on how much money to spend on your next investments based on historical data, or based on trends or what can happen next, or what most logical move you can make next based on data and the trend of things. We talked a little bit about conditions and the different types of conditions, but statistics relating to operations is a whole other video. Everything that you do operationally is data. That's how you need to look at it, from the amount of projects that your employees complete to the amount of data that you're accumulating with engagement, impressions, reach, and every client statistic from digital marketing to every KPI on the landing page that you have, all data that is collected is a statistic and as discussed in the conditions needs to be graphed, or line graph, statusized so you can check the condition of it. I can't mention this enough. It took me literally eight months to successfully transition into running my business on statistics because the reality of it was understood, but having one system called envisage.io where I had one employee upload all statistics from all employees that report on statistics to us which are all staff members, took us eight months. That being said, it's been the greatest change I've ever made. Having one dashboard that I can log into where I can look at every single thing that's going on in my business at one time, it's been the greatest thing that I've ever done in my company because a non-emotional allows me to look at a graph, see who I need to go out into my company and have accountability with. So if I see a down graph in a danger condition or an emergency condition, I go to that individual who manages that position and I simply ask him, hey, why are your stats down? I also go to the individuals when their graphs are up and I say, hey, I'm glad to see that your stats are up. What are you doing that's creating the success? Let's document it and create it into a policy so we can continue the ongoing success. It's important to run your business off of stats, both for your finances so you can have consistent growth and so you can always assess the current condition of your business and acting real time to adjust accordingly and the stats of your employees to hold them accountable and to see who you might need to fire, rehire or expand the division of because you're getting so many results there. Have one person in your business after you have an assistant role of some kind, gather all the stats from all your staff members, upload them to envisage.io for you to be able to log into and be able to check the stats in real time for everything in your business to non-emotionally assess where and what condition your business is in in order to take it to the next level. I look forward to seeing you in the next video. This has been Statistics under the Operations Section.
